Description: As a system user, I want to register an account, so that I can use the system functions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The user can enter registration information.
2. The system can create a new account successfully.
3. Duplicate accounts cannot be registered.
4. The system shows a success message after registration.
Notes: Supports account creation for system access.

Description: As a system user, I want to log in to the system, so that I can access functions related to my role.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The user can enter account and password to log in.
2. The system validates the login information.
3. The user enters the system homepage after successful login.
4. The system shows an error message when login fails.

Description: As a system user, I want the system to show pages according to my role, so that I can use the functions suitable for me.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system recognises Applicant, Module Organiser, and Admin roles.
2. Different roles enter different pages after login.
3. Users can only see functions related to their roles.
4. Unauthorised functions cannot be accessed.
Notes: Controls access based on user role.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to create my applicant profile, so that I can provide personal information for job applications.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can enter basic personal information.
2. The applicant can save the profile successfully.
3. Required fields must not be empty.
4. The system shows a confirmation message after saving.
Notes: Creates the core applicant profile record.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to edit my basic information, so that my personal information remains accurate.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can edit name, contact details, and other basic information.
2. Updated information is saved successfully.
3. The system shows an update success message.
4. The page displays the latest information.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to edit my skills information, so that I can show how my abilities match the job.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can add skills information.
2. The applicant can modify existing skills information.
3. Skills information is saved successfully.
4. The system shows an update success message.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to edit my availability, so that module organisers can understand my schedule.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can enter available working time.
2. The applicant can update existing availability.
3. Availability information is saved successfully.
4. The system shows an update success message.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to upload my CV, so that the module organiser can review my qualifications.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can upload a CV.
2. The uploaded CV is linked to the profile.
3. The system stores the CV successfully.
4. The system shows a success message after upload.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to view my uploaded CV information, so that I can confirm whether my CV has been submitted.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can view the current uploaded CV.
2. The displayed information matches the current user.
3. The CV information can be loaded correctly.
4. The page displays the information clearly.
Notes: Allows applicants to review submitted CV information.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to replace my uploaded CV, so that I can submit the latest version.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can upload a new CV again.
2. The system keeps the latest version as the current CV.
3. The updated CV remains linked to the profile.
4. The system shows a replacement success message.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to view a list of available jobs, so that I can find positions I can apply for.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can see a list of open jobs.
2. Each job shows title, requirements, workload, and deadline.
3. Closed or expired jobs are not shown as open jobs.
4. The job list loads successfully.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to view job details, so that I can understand the requirements before applying.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can open a job details page.
2. The page shows description, skill requirements, workload, and deadline.
3. The displayed content matches the selected job.
4. The details page can be opened from the job list.
Notes: Shows detailed information for a selected job.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to search jobs by keyword, so that I can quickly find relevant positions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can enter keywords to search jobs.
2. The system shows matching jobs.
3. The system shows a message when no matching result is found.
4. Search results only include available jobs.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to submit a job application, so that I can be considered for the position.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can submit an application for a selected job.
2. The system creates an application record successfully.
3. The application is linked to the correct job.
4. The system shows a success message after submission.

Description: As a system user, I want the system to record the application submission time, so that the application process information is preserved.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system records the time when an application is submitted.
2. The submission time is linked to the correct application.
3. The submission time can be read correctly.
4. The saved submission time is not lost.

Description: As a system user, I want the system to prevent the same applicant from applying for the same job more than once, so that application records remain valid.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system checks whether the applicant has already applied for the job.
2. Duplicate applications are blocked.
3. The system shows an error message.
4. The existing application record is not modified.

Description: As a system user, I want the system to check whether the applicant has created a profile before applying, so that application information is complete.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system checks whether a profile exists before submission.
2. The user cannot submit an application without a profile.
3. The system prompts the applicant to complete the profile first.
4. Users with profiles can continue the application.

Description: As a system user, I want the system to check whether the applicant has uploaded a CV before applying, so that application materials are complete.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system checks whether a CV exists before submission.
2. The user cannot submit an application without a CV.
3. The system prompts the applicant to upload a CV first.
4. Users with a CV can continue the application.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to view my application list, so that I can track all job applications in one place.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can view their own application list.
2. Each record shows the job title.
3. Each record shows the current status.
4. Only the current applicant's records are shown.
Notes: Shows all applications for the current applicant.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to view the status details of each application, so that I can understand the result.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant can view the status of a single application.
2. Status information is displayed clearly.
3. The status matches the correct application.
4. The page loads successfully.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to see the updated application result from the module organiser, so that I know whether I have been accepted.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The applicant view shows the latest status after the module organiser updates it.
2. Status values are displayed consistently.
3. The applicant can see updates without resubmitting the application.
4. The displayed information is accurate.
Notes: Reflects updated application results to applicants.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to create a job posting, so that TA applicants can apply for the job.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can create a new job.
2. The system allows entry of job title and description.
3. The job is saved successfully.
4. The created job appears in the list.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to set skill requirements for a job, so that more suitable applicants can be selected.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can enter skill requirements for a job.
2. The requirements are saved successfully.
3. The job details page shows the requirements.
4. Applicants can view the requirements.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to set the workload and deadline of a job, so that the job arrangement is clear.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can enter workload information.
2. The module organiser can set a deadline.
3. The information is saved successfully.
4. The applicant view shows the information correctly.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to edit job posting information, so that I can update recruitment content.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can edit an existing job.
2. Updated content is saved successfully.
3. Applicants can see the latest information.
4. The system shows a success message after update.

Story Name: View Applicant List for a Job
Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to view the applicant list for a job, so that I can see who has applied.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can view the applicant list for a selected job.
2. The list shows applicant names and basic information.
3. The list is linked to the correct job.
4. The list loads successfully.
Notes: Shows applicants for a selected job.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to view an applicant's basic information, so that I can make an initial evaluation.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can view the applicant's basic profile.
2. The displayed information belongs to the selected applicant.
3. The information is clear and readable.
4. The page displays successfully.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to view an applicant's skills and availability, so that I can judge whether the applicant matches the job requirements.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can view the applicant's skills.
2. The module organiser can view the applicant's availability.
3. The displayed information matches the selected applicant.
4. The information is complete.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to view an applicant's CV, so that I can evaluate the candidate more completely.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can view the applicant's CV.
2. The CV is linked to the correct applicant.
3. The CV content can be loaded correctly.
4. The page displays clearly.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to accept an applicant, so that I can complete a hiring decision.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can change the application status to Accepted.
2. The updated status is saved successfully.
3. The applicant view shows Accepted.
4. The result matches the correct application.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to reject an applicant, so that I can complete recruitment screening.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can change the application status to Rejected.
2. The updated status is saved successfully.
3. The applicant view shows Rejected.
4. The result matches the correct application.

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to set an application status to Pending, so that I can keep the candidate before making a final decision.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The module organiser can set the application status to Pending.
2. The updated status is saved successfully.
3. The applicant view shows Pending.
4. The result matches the correct application.

Story Name: View Assigned Workload of Each TA
Description: As an administrator, I want to view the assigned workload of each TA, so that I can understand the current allocation.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The administrator can view the workload of each TA.
2. The workload is linked to accepted jobs.
3. The data is displayed clearly.
4. The page loads successfully.

Description: As an administrator, I want to view the overall workload of TAs, so that I can avoid overloading individual TAs.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system can summarise the workload of each TA.
2. The overall workload information is displayed clearly.
3. The administrator can identify TAs with high workload.
4. The data is accurate.

Description: As a system developer, I want all data to be stored in CSV/JSON files, so that the system remains compatible with existing school systems.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. All user data stored in .json or .csv format
2. No database (MySQL, etc.) is used
3. Files can be backed up by simply copying
4. File format is documented in README

Description: As a system user, I want my password to be stored securely, so that my personal information is protected.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Passwords are not stored in plain text
2. Use hashing before saving to file
3. Login validates against hashed password
4. Error messages don't reveal whether username or password was wrong

Description: As a TA applicant, I want form validation to show errors immediately, so that I can correct mistakes before submitting.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. Required fields show warning if left empty
2. Email format is validated
3. Error messages are clear and specific
4. Form data is preserved when errors occur

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want the applicant list to load quickly even with many applicants, so that I can efficiently review candidates.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. List of 50+ applicants loads in <3 seconds
2. Sorting and filtering respond in <1 second
3. System remains responsive during data loading

Description: As a system user, I want clear error messages when something goes wrong, so that I understand what happened and what to do next.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. File read/write errors show user-friendly message
2. Network/timeout errors are caught
3. Errors are logged for debugging
4. System doesn't crash on unexpected errors

Description: As a Module Organiser, I want to see a skill match score for each applicant, so that I can quickly identify the most suitable candidates for a job.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system compares job skill requirements with the applicant's skills
2. A match score (e.g., 0-100%) is displayed next to each applicant
3. The score updates automatically when skills or requirements change
4. Higher scores are visually highlighted
Notes: AI-assisted feature; requires skill comparison logic.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want to see which skills I am missing for a job before I apply, so that I can decide whether to apply or improve my profile first.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. When viewing a job, the system highlights missing skills from the applicant's profile
2. Missing skills are listed clearly
3. The applicant is not blocked from applying
4. The feature is available for all open jobs
Notes: AI-assisted feature; helps applicants improve self-awareness.

Description: As an administrator, I want to receive a warning if a TA's total assigned workload exceeds a threshold, so that I can avoid overloading individuals.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. The system calculates total workload from all accepted jobs per TA
2. A configurable threshold (e.g., 20 hours/week) is used
3. A warning icon or message appears in the admin view
4. The warning does not block assignment but provides visibility
Notes: Supports workload balancing; configurable threshold is a plus.

Description: As a TA applicant, I want the system to suggest jobs that match my skills, so that I don't have to search manually for suitable positions.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. On the TA homepage, a list of recommended jobs is shown
2. Recommendations are based on skill match with job requirements
3. Recommended jobs are open and not yet applied to
4. The list updates when skills or jobs change

Description: As an administrator, I want to export the TA workload summary to a CSV file, so that I can share it with academic coordinators.
Acceptance Criteria:
1. A button "Export to CSV" is available on the admin workload page
2. The exported file contains TA name, total hours, and assigned jobs
3. The file format is CSV and can be opened in Excel
4. The export includes all TAs with accepted jobs
Notes: Enhances administrative usability; no AI needed.
